response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r0c2 is in critical distress: islanded with SoC at 38.7% (15.08/39 kWh) and a 1.0 kW load consuming ~2.6 hours of remaining capacity. The memory log shows a consistent pattern over the past 45 minutes—all peers (r0c1, r0c3, r1c2) are simultaneously requesting energy with urgent priority, while r0c2 itself has been rejecting all inbound requests due to its own SoC falling below the 60% preservation threshold. This indicates cluster-wide energy depletion in islanded mode with no visible generation or grid restoration. The prior balanced policy has proven unsustainable; the conservative shift to a 60% buffer was appropriate but is now insufficient given the accelerating decline. r0c2 must immediately pivot to self-preservation mode: cease all outbound sharing, escalate its own urgent request for inbound energy to any available source (grid restoration, microgeneration, or peer sacrifice), and prepare for load-shedding if SoC drops below 20%. The repeated rejections from peers suggest they are also in conservation mode, making peer-to-peer recovery unlikely without external intervention.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.75
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded cluster-wide depletion detected. r0c2 SoC 38.7% with ~2.6h load continuity. All peers requesting urgent energy; no inbound acceptance observed. Shifting to zero-share mode and escalating own urgent request. Prepare load-shedding threshold at 20% SoC."
ttl_ticks: 3
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
